content: LTBI diagnosis: positive IGRA (QuantiFERON-TB Gold, T-SPOT.TB) or TST (PPD) with no evidence of active disease. IGRA preferred for BCG-vaccinated individuals. Active TB diagnosis: 1) Clinical presentation; 2) Chest radiograph (upper lobe infiltrates, cavitation); 3) Microbiologic confirmation via acid-fast bacilli smear microscopy, nucleic acid amplification test (NAAT/Xpert MTB/RIF for rapid detection and rifampin resistance), and mycobacterial culture (gold standard, allows drug susceptibility testing). Three sputum samples collected ≥8 hours apart, at least one early morning. Extrapulmonary TB requires site-specific sampling (CSF, pleural fluid, tissue biopsy). Drug susceptibility testing critical for all isolates given rising resistance.
